pick.rationale: This Final Four matchup features two nearly identical elite teams—Michigan ranks #2 and Arizona #3 in both KenPom and Torvik with efficiency margins separated by just 0.82 points per 100 possessions—but Arizona holds decisive edges in health and momentum. Michigan star Yaxel Lendeborg, the Big Ten Player of the Year, is playing through a low ankle sprain suffered in the Big Ten Championship loss to Purdue, while Arizona's Koa Peat has already returned from his February injury and scored 21 points in the Big 12 championship victory. Arizona enters on an 11-game winning streak with Big 12 Player of the Year Jaden Bradley anchoring an elite two-way roster, while Michigan's late-season stumble and injury concerns to their primary playmaker create enough uncertainty to give the Wildcats a narrow advantage in this heavyweight showdown.

reasoningStep.title: Final Four: Arizona vs Michigan Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Analyzed two top-3 teams with nearly identical profiles, finding Arizona's health advantage and superior momentum as decisive factors.

evidence.detail: Michigan #2 (AdjEM +38.38, #2 defense, #8 offense); Arizona #3 (AdjEM +37.56, #3 defense, #7 offense). Efficiency gap of 0.82 points per 100 possessions.
Michigan's Yaxel Lendeborg (Big Ten POY) suffered low ankle sprain in Big Ten Championship vs Purdue, playing through injury. Arizona's Koa Peat returned from February injury earlier in tournament.
Arizona on 11-game winning streak, won Big 12 tournament championship. Michigan lost to Purdue in Big Ten Championship.
Both teams feature conference Players of the Year: Jaden Bradley (Big 12 POY) for Arizona, Yaxel Lendeborg (Big Ten POY) for Michigan.

toolCalls.summary: Fetched https://detroitsportsnation.com/yaxel-lendeborg-injury-update-michigan-ncaa-tournament/ddrysdale/college-sports/u-of-m-news/03/15/2026/484757/ and extracted 3000 characters of text.

result.text: Michigan Star Yaxel Lendeborg Reveals Injury Status for NCAA Tournament - Detroit Sports Nation About Editorial Disclaimer Ethics Policy Fact-Checking & Corrections Policy Privacy Policy About Editorial Disclaimer Ethics Policy Fact-Checking & Corrections Policy Privacy Policy Search Home Detroit Lions Lions Roster Detroit Tigers Detroit Red Wings Detroit Pistons College Sports Home Detroit Lions Lions Roster Detroit Tigers Detroit Red Wings Detroit Pistons College Sports Home » Michigan Star Yaxel Lendeborg Reveals Injury Status for NCAA Tournament Michigan Star Yaxel Lendeborg Reveals Injury Status for NCAA Tournament By: Don Drysdale Published: Mar 15, 2026, 6:56 pm Modified: Mar 15, 2026, 6:56 pm The Michigan Wolverines appear to have avoided a major injury scare just days before the start of the 2026 NCAA Men’s Basketball Tournament . According to reporter Alejandro Zúñiga , Michigan forward Yaxel Lendeborg revealed that he is dealing with a low ankle sprain , but he expects to play in the Wolverines’ NCAA Tournament opener. That is welcome news for a Michigan team that earned a No. 1 seed in the Midwest Region after posting an impressive 31–3 record during the regular season. A Key Piece of Michigan’s Title Push Lendeborg has been one of the most important players on Michigan’s roster this season. The 6-foot-9 forward has delivered strong production on both ends of the floor and is a major reason why the Wolverines are considered a legitimate national championship contender. During the 2025–26 season , Lendeborg averaged: 14.4 points per game 7.0 rebounds 3.3 assists 50.6% shooting from the field 34.3% from three-point range 82% from the free-throw line His all-around impact helped him earn 2025–26 Big Ten Player of the Year honors , along with All-Big Ten recognition . Why His Health Matters Michigan’s tournament hopes depend heavily on the versatility Lendeborg brings to the lineup. His ability to score inside, stretch the floor, and facilitate offense makes him one of the most complete forwards in the tournament field. If the injury had been more serious, it could have dramatically altered Michigan’s outlook in the bracket. Instead, the early indications suggest the Wolverines will have their star available when March Madness begins. Leave a Reply Cancel reply Your email address will not be published. Michigan Star Yaxel Lendeborg Reveals Injury Status for NCAA Tournament - Detroit Sports Nation About Editorial Disclaimer Ethics Policy Fact-Checking & Corrections Policy Privacy Policy About Editorial Disclaimer Ethics Policy Fact-Checking & Corrections Policy Privacy Policy Search Home Detroit Lions Lions Roster Detroit Tigers Detroit Red Wings Detroit Pistons College Sports Home Detroit Lions Lions Roster Detroit Tigers Detroit Red Wings Detroit Pistons College Sports Home » Michigan Star Yaxel Lendeborg Reveals Injury Status for NCAA Tournament Michigan Star Yaxel Lendeborg Reveals Injury Status for NCAA Tournament By: Don Drysdale Published: Mar 15, 2026, 6:56 pm Modified: Mar 15, 2026, 6:56 pm The Michigan Wolverines appear to have avoided a major injury scare just days before the start of the 2026 NCAA Men’s Basketball Tournament . According to reporter Alejandro Zúñiga , Michigan forward Yaxel Lendeborg revealed that he is dealing with a low ankle sprain , but he expects to play in the Wolverines’ NCAA Tournament opener. That is welcome news for a Michigan team that earned a No. 1 seed in the Midwest Region after posting an impressive 31–3 record during the regular season. A Key Piece of Michigan’s Title Push Lendeborg has been one of the most important players on Michigan’s roster this season. The 6-foot-9 forward has delivered strong production on both ends of the floor and is a major reason why the Wolverines are considered a legitimate national championship contender. During the 2025–26 season , Lendeborg averaged: 14.4 points per game 7.0 rebounds 3.3 assists 50.6% shooting from the field 34.3% from three-point range 82% from the free-throw line His all-around impact helped him earn 2025–26 Big Ten Player of the Year honors , along with All-Big Ten recognition . Why His Health Matters Michigan’s tournament hopes depend heavily on the versatility Lendeborg brings to the lineup. His ability to score inside, stretch the floor, and facilitate offense makes him one of the most complete forwards in the tournament field. If the injury had been more serious, it could have dramatically altered Michigan’s outlook in the bracket. Instead, the early indications suggest the Wolverines will have their star available when March Madness begins. Leave a Reply Cancel reply Your email address will not be published. 4. **Tournament Path**: Arizona beat Arkansas and Purdue to reach the Final Four. Michigan beat Texas Tech and Iowa State (without Keshon Gilbert).

This is an exceptionally close matchup between two elite teams with nearly identical profiles. The slight edge goes to Arizona due to Lendeborg's ankle injury affecting Michigan's star player, Arizona's superior momentum, and the fact that Arizona has been healthier throughout the tournament run.

- Michigan: #2 in both metrics, +38.38 efficiency margin, #2 defense, #7-8 offense

The key differentiating factors:
1. **Injury status**: Michigan's Yaxel Lendeborg (Big Ten Player of the Year) is playing through a low ankle sprain suffered in the Big Ten Championship. This is significant because he's their star player. Arizona's Koa Peat has already returned from his injury and has been effective.
